patches.summary: Added Personal Access Token auth wiring (X-Figma-Token header) to internal/config/config.go and internal/client/client.go.
patches.reason: The Figma OpenAPI spec declares three security schemes (PersonalAccessToken, PlanAccessToken, OAuth2). The printing-press generator emitted only the OAuth2 Bearer path, so PAT users were forced to set FIGMA_OAUTH2 with their PAT — which Figma's API rejects (PATs require the X-Figma-Token header, not Authorization Bearer). Patched config.go to also read FIGMA_ACCESS_TOKEN/FIGMA_API_TOKEN/FIGMA_API_KEY into a new FigmaPAT field, and client.go to detect the PAT marker and route it to the X-Figma-Token header. Doctor confirms 'auth_source: env:FIGMA_API_TOKEN' end-to-end. Tracked upstream as a generator multi-scheme-auth selection bug.
patches.validated_outcome: Live test against api.figma.com with a real PAT: 'figma-pp-cli files get abc' returns HTTP 404 (file not found, but auth accepted) instead of 401 (auth missing). Phase 5 dogfood: 152/165 (92%) of live tests passed.

patches.summary: Sort query-param keys before building the cacheKey hash in internal/client/client.go.
patches.reason: The generator-emitted cacheKey iterated over the params map without sorting, so Go's randomized map iteration order made the same set of params produce a different sha256 digest each call. This made the 5-minute response cache miss almost every time on multi-param requests — agents repeatedly hammering an endpoint never benefited from the cache. Sort keys first so equivalent requests collide. Greptile P1 on PR #380.

patches.summary: Use the actual auth-header name (Authorization vs X-Figma-Token) in the --dry-run echo in internal/client/client.go.
patches.reason: Sister to pat-auth-wiring: when AuthHeader() returns an X-Figma-Token-prefixed value, the live request path correctly routes it to that header. The dryRun branch hardcoded 'Authorization' as the label, so --dry-run output misled PAT users into thinking the request would carry an Authorization header. Mirror the prefix detection so the label matches the live header. Greptile P2 on PR #380.
